title: chaenorhinum rubrifolium subsp. raveyi
intro: Chaenorhinum rubrifolium subsp. raveyi is a subspecies of the Chaenorhinum rubrifolium plant, a flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it useful for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and soothing respiratory tract irritations. Herbal preparations of Chaenorhinum rubrifolium subsp. raveyi typically involve drying the plant's leaves and flowers, then infusing them in hot water or using them in teas and tinctures.
